document.title: Risk Management Plan for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management plan for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Establishes and documents the manufacturer's risk management plan, including planned activities, responsibilities, risk acceptability criteria, residual risk evaluation, verification, and production/post-production information review.
keyPoints: The plan applies to all stages of the life cycle of the A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW.
The devices are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
Risk acceptability uses a severity by probability matrix with risk levels 1-4 acceptable and 5-25 unacceptable.
Overall residual risk is evaluated after risk control measures have been implemented and verified.
Production and post-production information is collected from internal and external sources and reviewed for new or changed hazards.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

concepts.name: Risk management plan
concepts.evidence: This plan includes the following: the scope of the planned risk management activities... assignment of responsibilities and authorities... requirements for review of risk management activities... criteria for risk acceptability...

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: If the plan changes during the life cycle of the medical device, a record of the changes shall be maintained in the risk management file.

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: We define and document the policy for determining criteria for risk acceptability;

concepts.name: Probability of occurrence of harm
concepts.evidence: The likelihood that a hazardous situation will lead to harm is influenced by the life-cycle of the medical device and estimated number of devices in the market.

concepts.name: Severity
concepts.evidence: Severity levels are chosen and justified based on the harms that could result for a particular medical device.

concepts.name: Overall residual risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the manufacturer shall evaluate the overall residual risk posed by the medical device...

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: The get information method of post-production information should be collected according to the internal information and the external information.

concepts.name: Adverse event reporting
concepts.evidence: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency...

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance.

evidence.claim: The document is a Risk Management Plan for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Plan for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The product name is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The model names are A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Model name: A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The document revision is Rev.4 dated Apr/22/2024.
evidence.evidenceText: 4 Apr/22/2024 Added the evidence for estimating the probability of occurrence of harm.

evidence.claim: The plan was prepared, reviewed, and approved on April 22, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by Kim, Kihyun R&D team April 22, 2024 Reviewed by Lee, Dong jin R&D Manager April 22, 2024 Approved by Hur Namkyu Quality Manager April 22, 2024

evidence.claim: The plan applies to all life-cycle stages of the A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: This Risk management plan is applicable to all stages of the life cycle of the A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW.

evidence.claim: The device classification is IIb.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification IIb

evidence.claim: The expected service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Expected service life Approximately 5 years

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The detector uses TFT and CsI scintillator technology to convert X-ray data into digital signals.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI) and an electronic device that converts data read through it into digital signals.

evidence.claim: The risk acceptability policy uses two regions: acceptable risk and unacceptable risk.
evidence.evidenceText: All estimated risks are considered by its risk region and divided into two risk regions.

evidence.claim: Risk level is calculated as severity rating multiplied by probability rating.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk level = Severity rating × Probability rating

evidence.claim: Risk levels 1 through 4 are acceptable, and risk levels 5 through 25 are unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Level: 1~4 Green, Acceptable Risk (A) Acceptable region Risk Level: 5~25 Red, Unacceptable Risk (U) Unacceptable region

evidence.claim: Overall residual risk is evaluated after all risk control measures have been implemented and verified.
evidence.evidenceText: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the manufacturer shall evaluate the overall residual risk posed by the medical device...

evidence.claim: If overall residual risk is acceptable, significant residual risks are disclosed in accompanying documentation.
evidence.evidenceText: If the overall residual risk is judged acceptable, the manufacturer shall inform users of significant residual risks and shall include the necessary information in the accompanying documentation...

evidence.claim: Post-production information is collected from internal and external sources.
evidence.evidenceText: The get information method of post-production information should be collected according to the internal information and the external information.

evidence.claim: Internal post-production information sources include PMCF, CAPA reports, customer complaints, maintenance information, and customer satisfaction surveys.
evidence.evidenceText: Internal (1) PMCF (Post Market Clinical Follow up) (2) Corrective and preventive actions related reports (3) Customer Complaints (4) A / S recorded and maintained the information obtained during maintenance (5) Customer satisfaction surveys

evidence.claim: External post-production information sources include incident reporting, academic papers, expert opinions, similar device information, legal and standards trends, and clinical trial information.
evidence.evidenceText: External (1) Reporting medical incidents... (2) Related academic papers and expert opinions (3) Information related similar medical device (4) Establishment and revised trends in international and domestic laws / standards (5) In the process of performing clinical trials information

evidence.claim: The Quality manager is responsible for collecting and reviewing production and post-production information and managing quality problems.
evidence.evidenceText: Responsibility and the role of the Quality manager Collect, review the information and manage the quality problems in production and post-production phase

evidence.claim: The Quality manager performs risk analysis for collected information once every 12 months.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk analysis for the collected information (once every 12 months)

evidence.claim: Safety information and side effects are reported to the regulatory agency when necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text does not mention FDA; authority evidence in the text references European Union regulations and Korea medical device regulations.
The source path includes HnX, but the source text identifies the product models only as A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
Some text contains formatting or OCR-like spacing and wording issues, but key metadata and regulatory facts remain readable.
